quotes.verbatim_quote:
Here are eight sample licks that work well over major-type chords. Transpose them and
Start using them immediately in songs you are learning. These licks are labeled with major
7th chords but will work equally well with major 6th, major 9th and major I3th chords.
Experiment and let your ear be your guide.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines scope and transposition rule for the major licks.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
In this lesson, the chords you were working with in Lesson IA have been organized into
li-V7-I patterns. Each chord receives one beat. Be sure to transpose these progressions
to all twelve keys. Have patience, and enjoy the process. It can take some time to get
comfortable with these chord connections.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes ii-V7-I framework, one-beat-per-chord, transposition requirement.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Here are some sample licks to use over minor chords. Once again, learn, transpose and
insert these into your solos as soon as possible!
quotes.why_load_bearing: Encapsulates learn-then-apply pedagogy for minor chord licks.

quotes.verbatim_quote: By now, these chords are surely getting easier for you to handle. The following ii-V7-I progressions move across the string sets. Once again, each chord receives one beat. Transpose these to all twelve keys.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces shifting ii-V7-I across string sets to expand fretboard range.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Here are some licks that will work well over unaltered dominant chords. Try using them
over dominant 9th, | Ith and 13th chords as well. Transpose them and apply them to

your solos.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines applicability of dominant licks over 9th, 11th, 13th voicings.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Once the examples in Lesson 3A get easier, challenge yourself with these. They are arranged in one-measure ii-V7-I patterns. Take your time learning each example and then transpose them.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Raises difficulty by compressing ii-V7-I into one measure.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
There are many ways to improvise over min7’5 (half-diminished) chords. Here are just a
few licks to help you get used to the sound.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces half-diminished chord by alternate name as ear-training.
